mechanism_narrative: An exceptionally low federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate and stimulates borrowing-dependent corporate investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following months with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals weakening labor demand and falling household income expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp within 2 months, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production reflect coordinated demand-supply expansion that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, a pattern that does not hold when only one series increases.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO carries a t-statistic below 2.0 in predicting 20-day returns, or the long-only portfolio conditioned on both rising fails to outperform the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.25%, positive momentum in retail sales signals demand-driven recovery that translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days because ultra-low discount rates amplify equity valuation multiples.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, months of rising RSAFS do not produce positive S&P 500 20-day returns in at least 55% of instances, or the Sharpe ratio of the conditional long signal is below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.0% increases the cost of capital and debt service burdens sufficiently to depress near-term equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns following months with FEDFUNDS > 3% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment declines while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises, the joint deterioration confirms a labor market contraction that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days, an effect stronger than either signal in isolation.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fails to produce significantly negative 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals reduced manufacturing output and narrowing corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with month-over-month declining INDPRO do not coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand and downward earnings revision pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with falling RSAFS do not produce negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the mean return in those months is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
